title: Ceratochloa unioloides
taxonomy: Ceratochloa unioloides is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. The classification of Ceratochloa unioloides is as follows: it is classified in the genus Ceratochloa, in the tribe Andropogoneae, and in the family Poaceae. The genus Ceratochloa is a monotypic genus, meaning it contains only one species. This species is further classified as belonging to the order Poales.

intro: Ceratochloa unioloides, also known as the wild rice or rice grass, is a perennial grass species native to tropical Asia and the Pacific Islands. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it a traditional remedy for various ailments such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Ceratochloa unioloides include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Ceratochloa unioloides is often prepared as a decoction or tea, and it is also used in the form of a powder or capsule in modern herbal supplements.
